novel_features.name: The real going rate
novel_features.description: See the real going rate for an item in your area — median, 25th/75th percentile, min, max, and the firm-vs-negotiable split — computed across every listing you've pulled.
novel_features.rationale: No OfferUp page returns price statistics; it requires aggregating across many listings held in the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before buying or pricing a resale — it answers "what is this actually worth here" with a number instead of a scroll.

novel_features.name: Spot the underpriced finds
novel_features.description: Surface listings priced a chosen percentage below the local median for that item — the underpriced finds, ranked by how far under they are.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the computed local median joined against every stored listing — impossible from a single feed that just reshuffles results.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The deal-sniping command: it tells an agent which listings are underpriced right now, not just what exists.

novel_features.name: What's new since you looked
novel_features.description: Show only the listings that appeared since a cutoff for a saved search, so you never re-scan items you already saw.
novel_features.rationale: Requires tracking first-seen timestamps across sync runs in the local store; OfferUp only reshuffles its feed with no since-cutoff view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for a recurring watch — it answers "what dropped since I last looked" in one call.

novel_features.name: Catch price drops
novel_features.description: Detect listings whose price fell between syncs — the same item, now cheaper — sorted by the size of the drop.
novel_features.rationale: Requires per-listing price history across snapshots; no OfferUp page exposes prior prices for an item.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Catches sellers who just cut a price on something you're tracking — the moment to make an offer.

novel_features.name: Size up a seller
novel_features.description: Pull a seller's full synced inventory alongside their reputation badges (business/dealer/TruYou), join date, and the median asking price across their listings.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the seller record to all of their stored listings and aggregates — the seller and item pages never show inventory plus a price aggregate together.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Vet a dealer before buying to flip, or watch a high-volume seller's pricing in one view.

novel_features.name: Daily deal briefing
novel_features.description: A single composite report for a saved search combining what's new, what dropped in price, and what's underpriced.
novel_features.rationale: Composes three local-store reads no single page offers, returned as one agent-friendly payload.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The morning-ritual command and the natural single MCP tool call for an agent watching a market.

narrative.headline: Search local OfferUp listings from your terminal, keep them in a local database, and spot underpriced deals before anyone else — no login and no paid key for the things that matter most.
narrative.value_prop: OfferUp's web scrapers all dump a one-shot listing array and forget it. This CLI persists every listing in a local SQLite store, then layers on price intelligence no marketplace tool offers: price-check tells you the going rate in your area, deals flags listings below the local median, new-since and price-drops track saved searches over time, and seller-scan profiles a seller's whole inventory. Built for agents too — every command speaks --json, --select, and an MCP surface.
narrative.auth_narrative: Public commands need no login: search, item detail (listings get), seller lookup, category-scoped browse, and every price-intelligence command (price-check, deals, new-since, price-drops, digest, seller-scan). Set location with --zip (or --lat/--lon), not a credential. Account commands act on your own OfferUp account and require login: account (your profile), my-listings (plus archived, mark-sold, archive), saved, and messages (plus messages read). Auth is your OfferUp web session cookie, captured once via 'auth login --chrome' (the press-auth companion opens a controlled Chrome window; the session is encrypted at rest), checked with 'auth status', and cleared with 'auth logout'. The mutating commands my-listings mark-sold and my-listings archive preview by default and apply only with --confirm. Creating a new listing is not supported — OfferUp's web app posts Jobs only, so listing creation is mobile-app-only.

quickstart.comment: Health check — confirms the CLI can reach OfferUp; needs no auth.
Search live listings near a ZIP with no login.
See the local going rate; this fetches and stores listings itself, no separate sync needed.
Flag listings at least 25% under the local median.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty results for a common item
troubleshoots.fix: Widen the area with a larger --radius or a different --zip, or drop extra keywords from the query.

troubleshoots.symptom: Requests slow down or start failing in bursts
troubleshoots.fix: OfferUp throttles rapid requests; lower --limit/--max-pages and let the built-in pacing back off — the CLI rate-limits per request.

troubleshoots.symptom: Results are for the wrong city
troubleshoots.fix: Location defaults to IP geo; pass --zip (or --lat/--lon) explicitly to scope to your area.
Run sync for that query first — the price commands read the local store, not the live feed.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or user needs local-marketplace data from OfferUp: searching listings near a ZIP, pulling an item's full detail, vetting a seller, or — its real edge — answering price questions (what's the going rate, what's underpriced, what dropped) over a local store of listings. All of that works with no login. When logged in (auth login --chrome), it also manages your own account: your listings (view active and archived, mark sold, archive), saved lists, and messages. It does not create listings (mobile-app-only) or send messages.

recipes.title: Narrow a verbose search for an agent
recipes.explanation: Listing payloads are wide; --agent with --select returns only the fields an agent needs, saving context.

recipes.title: Find this week's underpriced finds
recipes.explanation: Lists Aeron chairs priced at least 30% below the local median around Phoenix.

recipes.title: Daily watch on a saved search
recipes.explanation: One call returns what's new, what dropped in price, and what's a deal for road bikes since yesterday.

recipes.title: Vet a seller before buying to flip
recipes.explanation: Shows the seller's badges, join date, full inventory, and median asking price in one view.

trigger_phrases: what's the going rate for X on OfferUp
any new OfferUp listings for X
